title: Fudd's Grimoire — Loot Table
description: A single DnD 5e loot table. One file = one table. Use item_ref to reference items defined in .data/items/ catalog files.

id.description: Unique snake_case identifier for this table. Used by other tables via 'reference: <id>'.

description.description: Human-readable description of when or where this table is used.

min_level.description: Minimum character level this table is balanced for.

max_level.description: Maximum character level this table is balanced for.

rolls.description: How many times to roll on this table when it is triggered. Can be a fixed integer or a dice expression.

tags.description: Tags for filtering and categorising tables (e.g. 'shop', 'dungeon', 'boss', 'tier1').

entries.description: The list of possible outcomes when this table is rolled. Each entry must have exactly one of: item_ref (catalog item), item (inline one-off), or reference (nested table roll).

Rarity.description: The DnD 5e rarity tier of the item.

ItemType.description: The broad category of the item.

DamageDice.description: A damage dice expression. Both dice notation and damage type are required.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: Flavour text or mechanical description of the item.

value_gp.description: Gold piece value of the item (DMG suggested prices).

attunement_requirements.description: Any specific attunement requirement, e.g. 'by a spellcaster'.
examples: by a creature of good alignment

magical.description: Whether this item is considered magical for the purposes of spells like Detect Magic and overcoming resistance. Defaults to false for mundane items; set true for any magic item.

weapon.description: Weapon-specific properties. Only applicable when type is 'weapon'.

range_normal_ft.description: Normal range in feet for ranged or thrown weapons.

magic_bonus.description: Attack and damage roll bonus (+1, +2, or +3 magic weapon).

armor.description: Armor/shield-specific properties. Only applicable when type is 'armor' or 'shield'.

dex_bonus.description: How Dexterity modifier applies to AC.

strength_requirement.description: Minimum Strength score required to wear without speed penalty.

stealth_disadvantage.description: Whether the armor imposes disadvantage on Stealth checks.

magic_bonus.description: AC bonus for magic armor (+1, +2, or +3).

consumable.description: Consumable stats for single-use items (potions, scrolls). The item is destroyed on use. For rechargeable charged items (wands, staves, rings, rods) use the 'wondrous' block instead.

charges.description: Number of charges or uses before the item is expended.

spell.description: The spell contained in the scroll or triggered by this item.

spell_level.description: Level of the contained or triggered spell (0 = cantrip).

wondrous.description: Properties for rechargeable charged magic items: wands, staves, rods, rings, and other wondrous items with limited uses. Use 'consumable' instead for single-use items (potions, scrolls).

charges.description: Maximum number of charges the item can hold.

recharge.description: How the item regains charges. Use a dice expression for a partial daily recharge (e.g. '1d6+1'), 'dawn' for a full recharge at dawn, or 'never' for a one-time-use item that crumbles when empty.

activation.description: The action economy cost to activate the item.

effects.description: List of spells, abilities, or effects the item can produce, optionally with charge costs.

currency.description: Currency denomination. Use when type is 'currency'. The quantity of coins is set by the 'quantity' field in the loot table entry.

tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering and categorisation.

LootEntry.description: One possible outcome in a loot table roll. Use exactly one of: item_ref (catalog item), item (inline one-off), or reference (nested table roll).

item_ref.description: ID of a reusable item defined in a .data/items/ catalog file (e.g. 'iron_sword'). Resolved by the app loader at runtime. Cross-file existence cannot be validated by JSON Schema — the loader enforces it.

item.description: Inline one-off item definition. Use only for items unique to this specific table. Prefer item_ref for anything reused across multiple tables.

reference.description: ID of another loot table to roll on instead (nested table roll).

weight.description: Relative probability weight. Higher = more likely to be rolled.

quantity.description: How many of this item the player receives. Fixed integer or dice expression.

tags.description: Override or supplement tags for this specific entry.
